run_summary: Screen 1 is holding 5 existing positions (3 near window expiry at 13 days, 2 fresh at 0 days) and evaluating 6 new flags from three distinct catalysts. The portfolio is concentrated in the Claude Tag / Slack AI trigger (3 flags: ONTO, AEIS, CGNX already held; PLAB new). ONTO and AEIS are conviction-eligible but the basket rule applies — both ONTO and AEIS are already held and at day 0, conflicting with the 5-15 day thesis window. PLAB enters as a new name from the same Claude Tag trigger and meets conviction criteria (threat_assessment='none', filings evidence explicit), but basket rule applies across the 4-name Claude Tag set (ONTO, AEIS, CGNX, PLAB), requiring PLAB to be exploratory. DOCN (OpenAI chip trigger) and PHR (loopy agents trigger) are singletons and qualify for their respective tiers.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: Photomasks are required for every semiconductor design release; AI chip proliferation increases design activity (Photronics explicitly notes demand depends on design activity, not just sales volume). A Slack AI integration has no mechanism to reduce photomask demand. The sharp drop is pure semi-sympathy noise.
entries.what_kills: A major customer (top 2 = 29% of revenue) cutting orders; consolidation in semiconductor customer base reducing design diversity.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment 'none', panic_calibration 'unjustified'. Filings evidence strong: no AI competition mention in risk factors, photomasks are required inputs for every chip design. Slack AI integration is mechanically unrelated to photomask demand. However, BASKET RULE applies: four names (ONTO, AEIS, CGNX now held; PLAB new) share the Claude Tag trigger. Four names from one trigger = basket bet on the trigger read, not individual names. Defaulting PLAB to exploratory (6%) rather than conviction, even though it individually qualifies (threat 'none', strong filings, conf 4). This keeps the Claude Tag basket exploration bounded at ~6% per name, freeing capital for follow-on trades.

red_team.weakest_link: The portfolio pass explicitly invokes a basket rule to cap conviction at exploratory tier despite rating the individual thesis as 'none' threat and confidence 4, creating a structural disconnect between the thesis quality and the position sizing.
red_team.critique: The individual thesis is sound: photomasks are structural inputs regardless of AI chip proliferation; Slack AI integration is mechanically unrelated to photomask demand. The five-day move of -3.63% is modest and volume multiple of 0.5 suggests passive de-risking rather than conviction selling. The real weakness is not in the thesis but in the meta-decision to limit position size to exploratory (6%) on the basis of basket-rule portfolio logic rather than the thesis itself. This is a portfolio-construction decision, not a fundamental weakness in PLAB's bull case. The thesis survives on filings and mechanism grounds, though the position size may be correct given Claude Tag trigger correlation risk.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
entries.thesis: OpenAI building its own inference chip is a vertical-integration story aimed at hyperscale AI inference economics — it has essentially zero direct read-through to DOCN's developer/SMB cloud business. DOCN is being lumped into 'cloud infrastructure providers at risk' by the trigger taxonomy, but its customer base and workload mix don't overlap with what Jalapeno disrupts. The sharp single-day drop on an already-extended week down looks like thematic AI-infra de-risking rather than a justified threat reaction.
entries.what_kills: Disclosure that a meaningful chunk of DOCN's AI/ML compute revenue comes from labs that might in-source (low probability given customer profile); guidance cut citing AI-infra competitive pricing; continued decline suggesting fundamental rather than sympathy selling.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OVERDONE classification, confidence 3 at screen minimum. Threat_assessment 'minimal' (developer/SMB customer base has no overlap with hyperscale AI inference in-sourcing). Panic_calibration 'unjustified' — 10-K confirms AI/ML as growth vector but no mechanism linking OpenAI custom-silicon vertical integration to DOCN revenue. Single-day move -5.47%, five-day -14.69% reflects thematic infra de-risking, not fundamental impairment. Exploratory tier (6%) appropriate for confidence exactly at threshold and 'minimal' (not 'none') threat.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes 'minimal' threat from AI-infra competition, but does not examine whether DOCN's customer base (developers building AI apps) might migrate to hyperscaler inference platforms as those become cheaper and more mature.
red_team.critique: The thesis correctly identifies that OpenAI's custom chip doesn't directly threaten DOCN's SMB/developer cloud business in compute supply. However, the bull case conflates two separate risks: (1) DOCN losing customers to hyperscalers' inference offerings, and (2) DOCN losing to chip-specific supply competition. The five-day decline of -14.69% suggests market conviction that AI-workload gravity is shifting toward managed inference—precisely where DOCN cannot compete. The 10-K confirmation of AI/ML as a growth vector is neutral on whether that growth rate survives when customers consolidate spend onto hyperscaler platforms. The 'minimal threat' assessment rests on non-overlap with frontier labs, but does not address whether mid-market AI developers (DOCN's actual customer base) will migrate upmarket as inference becomes commoditized.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: ONTO sells process-control equipment to advanced-node fabs (including AI accelerator manufacturing). A Slack AI integration has zero bearing on wafer inspection demand; if anything, more AI infrastructure buildout means more advanced-node capacity and more demand for ONTO's tools. The sharp drop is pure semi-cap sympathy on an unrelated AI headline.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of AI capex digestion at leading-edge fabs; customer concentration risk materializing (a key customer cutting orders); Rigaku regulatory snag.
entries.reasoning: Flagged ticker already held in portfolio at day 0 (entered same day as flag). Skip new trade slot; thesis and position status tracked under position_decisions. New flag updates conviction (threat 'none', filings explicit, confidence 4) but position already established; no new BUY warranted.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: AEIS sells power conversion to both semiconductor fabs and AI data centers. The 10-K explicitly identifies AI as a demand driver they are scaling capacity to meet. A Slack AI integration is mechanically backwards as a bear catalyst — more enterprise AI deployment → more data-center power demand → more AEIS. The drop is pure sympathy.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of data-center buildout pause; semiconductor capex cycle peak signals.
entries.reasoning: Flagged ticker already held in portfolio at day 0. Skip new trade slot. Thesis and holding status tracked under position_decisions. New flag reinforces conviction but position already filled.

entries.name: Cognex Corporation
entries.catalyst: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack
entries.thesis: CGNX serves industrial QA on factory floors requiring deterministic latency and precision. A Slack AI teammate has zero overlap with industrial machine vision. While CGNX's filings do mention AI as a long-term competitive risk, today's selloff on an enterprise-collaboration news item is a sympathy fade, not a credible threat materializing.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of open-source vision platforms taking share at factory-floor accounts; weakness in the consumer electronics segment.
entries.reasoning: Flagged ticker already held in portfolio at day 13. New flag (confidence 3, threat 'minimal', confidence at threshold) would be exploratory, but position already established under prior flag. No new trade; position managed under position_decisions.

entries.name: Phreesia, Inc.
entries.thesis: Agentic swarms target generic enterprise workflow automation; Phreesia's intake/engagement business is gated by HIPAA, EHR/PM integration plumbing, payer-specific eligibility, and life-sciences network contracts. A 'loopy' agent swarm cannot drop into a Cerner/Epic integration or take over patient-payment processing. The sympathy drop on a sub-average-volume day looks thematic, not fundamental.
entries.what_kills: An EHR vendor (Epic/Cerner) announcing native agentic intake; a Phreesia customer-cohort or net-retention deterioration in next print; a new risk-factor amendment naming agentic AI specifically.
entries.reasoning: Flagged ticker already held in portfolio at day 0. New flag updates conviction (confidence 3, threat 'minimal') but position already entered. No new trade slot; thesis tracked under position_decisions.
